title: Will Iran's Desperation Lead to a New Era of Global Terrorism?
summary: The article analyzes Matthew Levitt's Foreign Affairs piece warning that after US and Israeli military operations eliminated Ali Khamenei, a weakened and humiliated Iranian regime has abandoned strategic restraint and is poised to unleash global terrorism. Iran's Quds Force is now outsourcing terrorist acts to organized crime networks and social media recruits, shifting from calculated proxy warfare to chaotic, indiscriminate violence driven by institutional desperation.
talking_points: Iran has shifted from calculated, precision terrorism as a foreign policy tool to chaotic, indiscriminate attacks driven by existential desperation
The Quds Force is outsourcing terrorism to organized crime networks and social media recruits, from Russian mafia to petty criminals, to complicate investigations and mask state involvement
Iran's remaining Revolutionary Guard leaders are driven by a spirit of vengeance and nihilism born from impending collapse, operating with a 'nothing to lose' mentality
The asymmetric security rule applies: defense agencies must succeed every time, while terrorists need to succeed only once
Iran's strategic goal is to inflict civilian casualties to break American public morale and make the political cost of war unsustainable
author: Redacția
